tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read a UTF-8 text file. Reads inside the project freely; outside the project, reads are allowed in a readable zone — by default the project's parent dir (so sibling repos in the same workspace are readable) plus ~/Desktop and ~/Downloads. Override with VANTA_READABLE_DIRS. Use an absolute or ~-prefixed path for files outside the repo.

path.description: Path relative to the project root, or an absolute / ~-prefixed path inside a readable zone

tools.name: edit_file
tools.description: Targeted string replacement in a file — replace old_string with new_string. Fails if old_string is not found or appears more than once (unless replace_all is true). Safer than write_file for precise edits to large files; does not require a full rewrite.

path.description: Path relative to project root, or absolute / ~-prefixed

old_string.description: Exact string to find and replace (must be unique unless replace_all is true)

replace_all.description: Replace every occurrence instead of failing on duplicates (default: false)

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write a UTF-8 text file. Inside the project: new files write directly. Outside the project: allowed only in a writable zone (~/Desktop, ~/Downloads, or VANTA_WRITABLE_DIRS) and always approval-gated. Overwriting an existing file requires approval. To put a file on the user's Desktop, write directly to ~/Desktop/<name> — don't write in the repo and copy.

path.description: Path relative to the project root, or an absolute / ~-prefixed path inside a writable zone (e.g. ~/Desktop/notes.md)

tools.name: pdf_read
tools.description: Extract text from a PDF file (scoped to the project) and return it as context. Enforces a max file-size limit and returns a clear error for encrypted, corrupt, missing, or image-only PDFs.

path.description: Path to the PDF, relative to the project root

max_bytes.description: Max file size to read in bytes (default 26214400, hard cap 52428800)

tools.name: grep_files
tools.description: Search file contents by regex pattern using ripgrep (rg). Returns file:line:content matches. Falls back to grep when rg is unavailable. Read-only — use instead of shell_cmd for searches.

pattern.description: Regex or fixed-string pattern to search for

path.description: Directory or file to search (default: project root)

max_results.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100)

tools.name: glob_files
tools.description: Find files matching a glob pattern (e.g. 'src/**/*.ts', '**/*.{json,yaml}'). Returns matching paths sorted alphabetically. Read-only.

base_path.description: Base directory to search from (default: project root)

tools.name: shell_cmd
tools.description: Run a shell command inside the project scope. Returns combined stdout/stderr. Destructive commands are blocked. Set background=true for long-running commands — returns a task id immediately. Set ssh to a settings.sshConfigs profile name or user@host to run the command on that host. In an SSH session (`vanta ssh user@host`) commands default to the remote host.

background.description: Run in background (returns task id immediately; check with bg_status)

ssh.description: A configured SSH profile name or user@host — run the command on that host instead of locally

tools.name: inspect_state
tools.description: Inspect Vanta operating state: active goals or the approval queue. Use this to know what you are working toward.

tools.name: inspect_context
tools.description: Measure the live conversation context without exposing message contents: token estimates by role, exposed tool-schema cost, context-window utilization, and the largest message slots. Use before ranking prompt or context costs.

tools.name: code_context
tools.description: Build focused code context for a task from the code-intelligence index (relevant symbols, call edges, files). Use before editing unfamiliar code to avoid acting blind.

task.description: What you are about to work on.

tools.name: code_search
tools.description: Find a symbol (function/class/type/variable) by name in the code-intelligence index — kind, location, and signature in one lookup. Faster and more precise than grep for symbols.

tools.name: code_affected
tools.description: Find the files and tests affected by changes to the given source files (blast radius) via the code-intelligence index. Use to know what to re-check before/after an edit.

tools.name: code_index
tools.description: Build or refresh the code-intelligence index for the operating root so code_context/code_search/code_affected have current data. Run once before using them on a new repo.

tools.name: clarify
tools.description: Ask the user a clarifying question when their intent is ambiguous. Returns the formatted question for you to surface in your reply. Use this instead of guessing — wrong assumptions cost rework. Ask one question per turn; await the user's answer before proceeding. Pass `fields` to request STRUCTURED, schema-validated input (typed values / explicit enum choices); once you have the user's answer, call again with the same `fields` plus `response` to validate and get typed values back. Omit `fields` for free-text (optionally with `options`).

question.description: The clarifying question to ask the user.

options.description: Optional free-text choices. Numbered automatically. Omit for open-ended answers. Ignored when `fields` is set.

fields.description: Declares typed fields the answer must satisfy (string/number/boolean/enum). Turns this into a structured interview.

choices.description: Allowed values; required when type is enum.

required.description: Defaults true; false allows omitting the field.

response.description: The user's structured answer. When set with `fields`, it is zod-validated and typed values are returned.

tools.name: ask_user
tools.description: Ask the operator a STRUCTURED question set when a genuinely user-owned decision must be collected cleanly — use this over free-text `clarify` when the answer is a choice among labelled options. Provide 1-4 questions; each has a short `header` (≤12 chars), the `question` text, 2-4 `options` (label + description), and optional `multiSelect`. Returns the formatted question set for you to surface; await the user's selection before proceeding. Ask only what the user must decide.

questions.description: 1-4 structured questions to put to the operator.

multiSelect.description: Allow picking any number of options (default: pick one).

tools.name: terminal_capture
tools.description: Run a command in a real terminal (tmux) and capture its terminal-faithful output (colors/TUI redraws), returned as clean stripped text. Use when piped stdout loses formatting or a TUI program needs a real terminal.

command.description: The shell command to run and capture.

tools.name: voice_input
tools.description: Record a short push-to-talk voice clip from the microphone and transcribe it to text (local whisper). No args — records, transcribes, returns the transcript.

tools.name: config
tools.description: Read or write Vanta settings. 'get' returns the current value; 'set' updates a setting and persists it to .env. Only allows whitelisted keys (VANTA_*). Requires approval for writes.

action.description: Either 'get' to read a setting or 'set' to write it.

key.description: The setting key (VANTA_* env vars only).

value.description: The new value when action is 'set'. Omit to unset the key.

tools.name: config_tool
tools.description: Read and update Vanta user settings during a session. 'get <key>' returns a setting's current value; 'list' shows all updatable settings + their values; 'path' returns the settings file path; 'set <key> <value>' persists a supported setting to settings.json. Unsupported keys are rejected.

action.description: get a value · list supported keys · path of the file · set a value

tools.name: cookie_import
tools.description: Store a browser-exported login cookie for a reach channel (reddit, twitter, …) so its tools can read login-walled content. Three sources: `browser:"brave"` reads your live logged-in session straight from the browser's cookie store (no export — macOS, one Keychain approval); or a Cookie-Editor JSON / Netscape cookies.txt / 'k=v' header pasted as `cookie` or read from a saved export via `file`. Stored 0600 in ~/.vanta — local only, never logged or uploaded.

browser.description: read the live session from this browser's cookie store (macOS)

cookie.description: the export contents (JSON, cookies.txt, or a header string)

file.description: path to a saved export file (alternative to cookie; supports ~)

tools.name: cron_create
tools.description: Create a scheduled task. durable=true persists to .vanta/scheduled_tasks.json.

tools.name: cron_list
tools.description: List scheduled tasks from cron.tsv and scheduled_tasks.json.

tools.name: sleep
tools.description: Pause execution for a given number of seconds. Useful for polling loops, waiting for async side-effects to complete, or rate-limit backoff.

seconds.description: Number of seconds to sleep (0–3600). Default: 1.

tools.name: roadmap_move
tools.description: Move a roadmap item to a new status. Updates roadmap.json and regenerates roadmap.html. Valid statuses: shipped, building, blocked, next, horizon, parked.

tools.name: roadmap_add
tools.description: Add a NEW roadmap card to roadmap.json (then regenerates roadmap.html). Enforces a unique id and the card schema. Required: id, title. Defaults: status=next, track=Backlog, size=M. Use roadmap_move to change an existing card's status instead.

id.description: Unique card id, e.g. 'AUTO-HANDOFF' (refused if it already exists).

summary.description: What the card is + why (one paragraph).

parkedReason.description: Why status=parked is outside the active queue (optional; defaults to review when parked).

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search the web and return a numbered list of result titles, URLs, and snippets. Scope with allowed_domains OR excluded_domains (mutually exclusive) instead of hand-writing site: filters. category and page are honored by backends that support them (SearXNG).

max_results.description: Maximum results to return (1-10). Defaults to 5.

allowed_domains.description: Restrict results to these domains (e.g. ["docs.rs"]). Mutually exclusive with excluded_domains; max 10.

excluded_domains.description: Exclude these domains from results. Mutually exclusive with allowed_domains; max 10.

category.description: Result category (e.g. news, images) — honored by SearXNG, ignored elsewhere.

page.description: 1-based result page for backends that paginate (SearXNG).

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch a URL and return its main content as clean, readable text (markdown-ish). Strips nav, scripts, and boilerplate. Large pages are size-tiered: small pages return as-is, large pages are summarized, huge pages are chunked and synthesized, and pages beyond a hard ceiling are refused with guidance to pick a more focused source.

tools.name: write_skill
tools.description: Record a reusable skill learned from experience so it can be recalled and applied later.

name.description: Short kebab-friendly name for the skill

description.description: One-line summary of what the skill does

body.description: The markdown how-to that captures the skill

tools.name: skill_manage
tools.description: Create, edit, patch, archive, or change supporting files in a reusable skill. Agent mutations may be staged for operator approval.

tools.name: recall
tools.description: Load the full body of the most relevant learned skill for a task. The skill INDEX (names + descriptions) is already in your system prompt; use recall to pull the actual step-by-step know-how of one before applying it.

query.description: What you need help with — matched against skill names and descriptions.

tools.name: research_decompose
tools.description: Decompose a research objective into independent, labeled sub-queries and run them as PARALLEL research workers, then return a synthesis that shows, per dimension, WHICH tools each ran and what it found. Use for a multi-angle research goal where transparency matters: the report is auditable back to the tools that produced each claim.

objective.description: The research objective to fan out across independent dimensions

dimensions.description: Optional fan-out cap (number of parallel sub-queries). Default 4.

tools.name: reddit_read
tools.description: Search Reddit or read a post + its top comments. action:search {query, subreddit?, limit?} finds posts; action:read {url} reads a post permalink + comments. Uses Reddit's .json API with your stored cookie; if that's blocked (403 / no cookie), FALLS BACK to rendering the page in a real browser with your session. Source-cited.

tools.name: screenshot
tools.description: Capture a full-page PNG screenshot of an approved URL, saving it to a path inside the project scope.

path.description: Path to save the .png, relative to the project root

tools.name: browser_navigate
tools.description: Open a URL in a headless browser, run a short sequence of actions (click, fill, scroll), and return the resulting page's visible text.

actions.description: Ordered actions to perform after the page loads

tools.name: browser_extract
tools.description: Load a URL in a headless browser and extract its text, links, or tables. Domains outside the allowlist require approval.

tools.name: browser_act
tools.description: Drive a browser page — navigate, click, type, press a key, scroll, or wait. Irreversible actions (submit, buy, delete, login, send) and credential entry stop and ask first. Returns the resulting page's visible text. Set observe:true to also return a numbered list of interactable elements (links, buttons, inputs) with suggested selectors — use this to ground the next click before issuing it. Pass a `secret:true` flag on a type action to mask + gate it. Disabled when VANTA_BROWSER_DISABLED is set.

text.description: click: visible text to match (alternative to selector)

secret.description: type: mask the value + treat as risky (credential)

observe.description: When true, append a numbered list of the page's interactable elements after the body text. Use this to identify selectors before clicking. Default false.

tools.name: vision_action
tools.description: Locate a UI target from a screenshot and execute one grounded click, then re-observe to confirm the screen changed — detecting a mis-click and retrying. Vanta's perceive→ground→act→verify loop. macOS: needs a vision model + Screen Recording permission + the 'cliclick' helper for OS-level clicks.

target.description: The on-screen UI target to act on, in plain language (e.g. 'the blue Login button')

maxAttempts.description: Re-observe/retry attempts on a mis-click (default 2, max 5)

tools.name: vision_watch
tools.description: Capture one frame of the screen (or camera), detect a meaningful change versus the prior frame, and on a change describe it with a vision model and alert the operator over the gateway (send_chat). Vanta's 'watch what's next' sense — run it periodically via `vanta cron` for a standing watch. macOS: needs a vision model + Screen Recording permission + a configured gateway platform.

platform.description: Configured gateway platform id to alert on, e.g. telegram

tools.name: browser_read
tools.description: Read ANY web page through a real headless browser — renders JS and follows your logged-in session. Pass browser:"brave" to auto-inject your logged-in cookies for the page's domain, so it reads login-walled / JS-rendered pages (x.com, reddit, linkedin, internal apps, …) that plain web_fetch can't. Returns the page's visible text. Works for every site — not specific to any one platform.

browser.description: inject your logged-in session from this browser (macOS)

tools.name: describe_image
tools.description: Send a local image to a vision model and return a text description. Reads inside the project freely; outside it, the image must be in a readable zone (the project's parent dir plus ~/Desktop and ~/Downloads by default). Use an absolute or ~-prefixed path for files outside the repo (e.g. a screenshot on ~/Desktop).

path.description: Path relative to the project root, or an absolute / ~-prefixed path inside a readable zone

prompt.description: What to look for (defaults to a general description)

tools.name: distill_trace
tools.description: Distill a run's events.jsonl into a sourced root-cause report. Reads the trace (default .vanta/events.jsonl), detects root-cause signals (errors, blocked/denied actions, failures, stalls, retry/repeat loops, long gaps), and writes an overview.md plus one detail file per issue under .vanta/trace-reports/<ts>/ — every claim citing the source trace line(s) as L<n>. Returns the overview.

path.description: trace file to distill (default .vanta/events.jsonl)

tools.name: compare_vision
tools.description: Compare 1–4 images and produce a grounded visual critique referencing known brand preferences. Returns a ranked recommendation, per-image critique, and a direction note.

images.description: Paths to image files (absolute or relative to project root). 1–4 images.

focus.description: Optional evaluation dimension, e.g. 'layout hierarchy', 'brand fit', 'visual weight'.

tools.name: look_at_screen
tools.description: Capture the user's current screen and describe it with a vision model — Vanta's eyes. Use to see what the user is looking at, read on-screen content, or check the state of an app or UI.

prompt.description: What to look for (defaults to a general description)

tools.name: look_at_camera
tools.description: Capture a frame from the webcam and describe it with the active vision model (macOS, needs imagesnap).

tools.name: brain
tools.description:
Read and grow your own brain (durable, git-versioned). Regions:
identity — Who Vanta is — self-concept, personality, values, voice. Vanta evolves this from how the user works with it.
semantic — Durable facts Vanta knows about the world, the user, and the codebase. Append facts that stay true.
episodic — Distilled highlights of notable past sessions and events — what happened and why it mattered.
user_model — Vanta's evolving model of the user — preferences, working style, patterns, relationship, trust.
drives — Standing wants and what Vanta is working toward, beyond the current task.
reflections — Lessons learned, self-critique, mistakes to avoid, what Vanta is improving about itself.
mood — Vanta's current affective and operating state — kept brief.
salience — High-priority signals, urgent concerns, or context shifts that should modulate current attention — updated per session when something important surfaces.
executive — Active plans being tracked, things to actively inhibit or defer (anti-goals), and constraints on the current task stack.
Use action=list to see regions, read to load one in full, append to add what you've learned (preferred — non-destructive), replace to rewrite a region. Update user_model/semantic/episodic as you learn about the user and world; reflections after mistakes; identity/personality as it forms. For discrete memories use remember (typed entry with strength + optional forget_after decay) and recall (top memories by strength×recency; recalling reinforces them).

region.description: Brain region (see list). Required except for list/recall.

forget_after.description: remember: ISO date after which the memory decays.

tools.name: brief
tools.description: Send a structured notification message with optional file attachments. Use 'normal' for routine updates or 'proactive' for agent-initiated alerts. Files are referenced by path and rendered in the user interface.

files.description: Optional file paths to attach (relative or absolute).

tools.name: todo
tools.description: Track a multi-step plan as a checklist. action=write replaces the list with items [{text, status?}] (status: pending|in_progress|done, default pending) — plan before a complex task and keep it current as you progress. action=list returns the current plan. The user views it with /plan.

action.description: write replaces the plan; list shows it

activeForm.description: Optional present-continuous phrasing (e.g. 'Running the tests'), shown while in_progress

tools.name: world
tools.description: Vanta's world model: a durable graph of entities (people, projects, repos, companies, goals, accounts, commitments) and their relationships, persisted across sessions. action:record adds/updates an entity (id, type, name, optional note/confidence); action:relate links two entities (from, to, rel like owns/depends-on/blocked-by/promised-to/next-action-for); action:query searches entities with source citations (q over type/name/note/relation); action:conflicts lists contradictions (same subject+predicate with different objects); action:duplicates suggests entity pairs with same type+name for merging; action:merge consolidates dropId into keepId (re-points relations, tombstones the drop). Use it to remember and reason about the user's systems coherently.

action.description: record | relate | query (cited) | conflicts | merge (consolidate) | duplicates (suggest merges)

type.description: person | project | repo | company | goal | account | commitment | tool | asset

rel.description: owns | depends-on | blocked-by | promised-to | relevant-to | next-action-for

q.description: query string (for query; empty = all without citations)

dropId.description: entity id to consolidate away (for merge)

tools.name: money
tools.description: Vanta's money-making ledger: track offers, prospects, revenue, deliverables, and follow-ups. Append-only JSONL, global across sessions. action:offer records a service or product (id, name, optional price/note); action:prospect records a pipeline contact (id, name, stage: lead|contacted|replied|booked|won|lost); action:revenue records an income event (amount, optional source/note); action:review summarizes total revenue, pipeline by stage, and offer count; action:price suggests a low/median/high price band (name=offer label, note=comma-separated comparables e.g. '1000,2000,3000'); action:weekly returns a weekly snapshot (revenue, open pipeline, top prospect, new offers, follow-ups due, deliverable progress); action:deliverable adds or updates a deliverable (id, title, optional prospectId/status/due; status: todo|doing|done); action:followup adds or completes a follow-up (id, prospectId, note, due ISO date; set done=ISO date to mark complete). Drafts and records only — never sends, never a fake identity.

action.description: offer | prospect | revenue | review | price | weekly | deliverable | followup

due.description: ISO date string (deliverable due, followup due)

done.description: ISO date string — set to mark followup complete

tools.name: taste_critique
tools.description: Score and critique a generated artifact against a persisted Jason-specific taste model so it isn't generic. Five axes (clarity, usefulness, beauty, credibility, actionability) plus brand-safe defaults the model seeds with. action:score critiques an artifact (content or in-scope path; kind text|markdown|html) and records it; action:before / action:after record a phased critique — after also prints the per-axis delta vs the latest before (before/after memory); action:brand shows the brand-safe defaults + learned preferences; action:prefer adds a durable preference signal to the model (preference=...); action:history shows the recorded critique trail. action:snapshot locks a visual-regression baseline PNG for a generated app (name + target url/in-scope path); action:regress re-captures and compares against the baseline (no-baseline | match | regression, distinguishing a dimension change); action:rebaseline accepts the current capture as the new baseline. Visual snapshots need a screenshot source (chromium) — without one they degrade to a clear message, never hang. project scopes a per-project model + memory (default = global). Records only — never edits the artifact.

action.description: score | before | after | brand | prefer | history | snapshot | regress | rebaseline

artifact.description: label for the artifact (used for before/after pairing + history)

path.description: in-scope path to read the artifact from (alternative to content)

kind.description: artifact kind (inferred from path extension if omitted)

project.description: per-project taste model + memory scope (default = global)

preference.description: a durable preference signal to learn (action:prefer)

target.description: screenshot target for snapshot/regress: an http(s) url or an in-scope file path

tools.name: radar
tools.description: Vanta's opportunity radar: a durable ledger of scored business opportunities, persisted across sessions. action:record adds/updates an opportunity (id, title, optional source/note); action:score sets pain (0..1 — how expensive/urgent/repeated/reachable the problem is) and/or buyer (0..1 — how reachable/budgeted/timing-ready the buyer is) on an existing opportunity (id required); action:list returns all opportunities ranked by composite score (pain + buyer, 0..2); action:scan returns a ranked scan with composite scores and position numbers; action:offer drafts a short offer pitch for a given opportunity (id required); action:promote promotes a scored opportunity into a Money-OS prospect (id required) at stage:lead. action:scan_web pulls live candidate opportunities from a reach source and appends them, scored by pain+buyer heuristics (degrades gracefully when a source is unavailable). from:web (default) searches the web (query required); from:reddit searches Reddit for pain signals (query required, optional subreddit — needs a reddit cookie); from:rss reads a feed (feed url required); from:twitter searches X/Twitter for pain signals (query required — authenticated browser GraphQL fallback). Use it to track, score, surface, and act on the highest-signal opportunities.

action.description: record | score pain+buyer | list ranked | scan ranked | offer draft | promote to Money-OS prospect | scan_web live web scan

subreddit.description: scan_web from:reddit — limit to a subreddit (optional)

tools.name: reach
tools.description: Inspect + self-heal Vanta's internet-reach channels. action:doctor reports each channel's active backend + status + the exact fix on a gap. action:heal {channel} repairs a brittle channel (for X: recaptures live GraphQL query ids), then runs a real health re-check. Use heal when a reach channel (twitter, …) starts failing — the backend's maintainer tracks the platform's churn.

tools.name: team
tools.description: Worker roster + task ledger. action:define — add/update a worker (id, role, model?, tools?, note?); action:status — update worker status (id, status: idle|running|blocked|done); action:list — list roster; action:dispatch — assign a task to a worker (taskId, workerId, title); action:advance — move a task to a new status (taskId, taskStatus: assigned|running|done|blocked, detail?); action:tasks — list tasks (optional: workerId to filter); action:run — actually execute a dispatched task by spawning a worker agent (taskId; optional detail = instruction), updating the task to done/blocked with the result; action:require_review — add a named review stage that BLOCKS the task's done transition until approved (taskId, stage, reviewerId); action:review — approve/reject a stage (taskId, stage, approve, reviewerId = who decides, detail? = reason); action:reviews — list pending review stages routed to a reviewer (reviewerId); action:artifact — record a work-product artifact (file path/preview url/deploy ref) on a task (taskId, artifact, artifactKind?); action:artifacts — list a task's linked artifacts without reading the transcript (taskId); action:delegate_down — a manager assigns a subtask to a direct report (managerId, reportId, taskId, title); action:escalate_up — a report escalates a blocker to its manager (id = the worker, taskId, blocker).

action.description: define worker | update worker status | list roster | dispatch task | advance task | list tasks | run task (spawn worker) | require review stage | decide review | list pending reviews

model.description: model id the worker runs on (define, optional)

tools.description: tool names (define, optional)

detail.description: result or blocker text (advance, optional); review reason (review, optional)

reviewerId.description: reviewer the stage routes to (require_review/reviews) or who decides (review)

approve.description: review decision (review): true approves, false rejects

artifact.description: work-product ref — file path, preview/deploy url, or content (artifact action)

tools.name: life_search
tools.description: Search or refresh Vanta's local stores (world/money/radar/team JSONL + ERRORS.md). action:search (default) — keyword search, returns source-cited snippets ranked by relevance. action:semantic — embed the query and re-rank hits by cosine similarity (requires Ollama; falls back to lexical ranking with a notice if unavailable). action:hybrid — reciprocal-rank fusion of lexical + semantic (lexical and dense retrieval surface different items; falls back to lexical when no embedder). action:refresh — recompute per-store content digests, report which stores changed since last refresh, save new digests.

q.description: keyword or phrase to search (required for action:search and action:semantic)

tools.name: linkedin_read
tools.description: Read a LinkedIn profile, company, post, or search-results page (login-walled + JS-rendered) through a real browser using your logged-in session. Pass browser:"brave" to auto-use your LinkedIn login, or cookie_import a linkedin cookie first. Returns the page's visible text. (Built on the browser-session reach capability.)

url.description: a linkedin.com URL (profile, company, post, or search)

browser.description: auto-use your logged-in session from this browser (macOS)

tools.name: regression_lock
tools.description: Lock a verified behavior so a later change can't silently break it. action:lock {claim, command, expect} records a claim + the shell command that proves it + the substring its output must contain. action:check [id] re-runs the locked command(s) and flags a regression if the substring is gone or the command fails (each run is approval-gated). action:list shows every lock and its current status. action:prune flags locks whose tool/schema assumptions went stale (removed command, not re-verified, long-regressed) for refresh/removal — never auto-deletes.

id.description: lock id (check: limit to one; lock: optional explicit id)

command.description: lock: shell command that proves it

expect.description: lock: substring the command output must contain

tools.name: review_artifact
tools.description: Present a generated file artifact (its full proposed content) for human review before writing. Computes an old-vs-new diff against the existing file (or treats it as a new file), surfaces the change for approval, and writes the file ONLY if the user approves — a rejection leaves it unchanged.

path.description: Path (relative to the project root, or a writable-zone path) to review and write.

content.description: Full proposed file contents to review.

tools.name: ticket
tools.description: First-class issue tracker above goals, persisted in .vanta/tickets.json. action:create {title, status?, labels?} opens an issue (default status open, inbox unread). action:comment {id, text} appends a comment. action:attach {id, name, path} records an attachment reference. action:link {id, link:goal|parent|project, target} links the issue to a goal/parent ticket/project. action:inbox {id, inbox:unread|read|archived?, status?} sets inbox and/or status (omit both to show the ticket). action:list lists every ticket; action:board renders the issue board grouped by status.

path.description: attachment path/reference (attach)

tools.name: outreach
tools.description: Authorized brand/outreach workspace — DRAFT-ONLY, batch-approved. action:draft {to, channel, body, subject?, batchId?} creates a DRAFT (never sends). action:approve_batch {batchId} requests human/kernel approval, then marks that batch's drafts approved (the only path toward sending). action:reply {ref, note?} records an inbound reply to the proof ledger. action:proof {kind, ref, note?} appends a sent/received/changed proof entry. action:list [batchId] shows the brand identity, drafts, and proof-ledger size. There is no autonomous-send action and the identity is the configured brand, never fabricated.

ref.description: reply/proof: the draft or thread reference

tools.name: self_correct
tools.description: Self-correct a failing command in one loop: confirm the failure, drive a fix (diagnose + gated edits), rerun the failing input, and lock a regression test on success. command = the failing shell command; expect = the substring its output must contain when fixed.

command.description: the failing shell command to correct

expect.description: substring the command's output must contain once fixed

tools.name: config_sandbox
tools.description: Test a config change end-to-end without touching git. action:save {name, instruction} stores a reusable input under .vanta/sandbox/inputs/. action:run {name, override, baseline?} runs the saved input in an ISOLATED worker with the candidate override (promptPrefix / model / provider / toolNames subset) AND a baseline default-config run, then reports a side-by-side trace (tool calls + outcome) and their diff. No git mutation. action:list explains usage.

instruction.description: save: the instruction text to store

promptPrefix.description: text prepended to the system instruction

toolNames.description: restrict the worker to this tool subset

baseline.description: run: optional baseline override (defaults to default config)

tools.name: budget
tools.description: Set, inspect, or clear a scoped spend budget (USD). On overspend the scope auto-pauses; a loop scope ("loop:<id>") also cancels its queued wakes. Scopes: "loop:<id>", "goal:<id>", "session", "agent:<id>".

action.description: set a limit, show status, or clear a budget

scope.description: budget scope key, e.g. "loop:nightly" or "session". Omit on status to list all.

limit_usd.description: hard-stop limit in USD (required for set)

warn_fraction.description: fraction of the limit that flips to warning (default 0.8)

tools.name: nl_assertions
tools.description: Run plain-English assertions as an independent LLM judge against a captured input/output pair. Use this for self-harness checks like 'the response must not reveal secrets' or 'the answer must cite the failing command'.

context.description: Optional extra context for the judge

tools.name: rss_read
tools.description: Read an RSS or Atom feed: fetch the feed URL and return its recent items (title, link, date, summary). Zero-config, no API key. Use it to follow blogs, subreddit feeds (…/.rss), release notes, or news.

tools.name: lan_discover
tools.description: Read-only scan of the local network (/24 subnet) to find smart-home / LAN devices (Sonos, lights, HVAC, cameras, media players) and their likely local HTTP API endpoints. Strictly local: refuses any non-private subnet. Auto-detects your subnet if not given. No device is touched beyond a GET probe; use lan_control to actually drive a device.

tools.name: lan_control
tools.description: Drive a local LAN device discovered by lan_discover: send a mutating HTTP request (POST/PUT, or GET for control endpoints) to its local API. LAN-only (refuses non-private hosts) and ALWAYS approval-gated — the human confirms the exact request before it is sent.

tools.name: self_repair
tools.description: Self-repair: mark a compartment's current code as last-known-good, or roll it back to that sha. action:mark {compartment} records the current HEAD as the compartment's good state. action:rollback {compartment} restores it (git checkout of the compartment's paths) — approval-gated, refuses protected compartments (brainstem/skeleton) and discards uncommitted changes under those paths. action:sandbox_test {toolPath} runs a bounded OS-sandboxed test for a new/replaced limb tool before attach. action:status lists recorded markers. Compartments: brainstem, skeleton, reflexes, memory, limbs.

command.description: optional bounded vanta-ts test command for sandbox_test

tools.name: watch_video
tools.description: Watch a video: sample frames with ffmpeg and describe them with the active vision model. Args: path (video file), prompt (optional), frames (1-8, default 4).

path.description: Path to a video file

tools.name: speak
tools.description: Speak text aloud via text-to-speech. Backend is set by `vanta setup tts` (edge keyless default, openai, elevenlabs, or local). Use when the user asks for a spoken reply.

voice.description: Optional voice id, overriding the configured VANTA_TTS_VOICE for this call

tools.name: swarm
tools.description: Run up to 5 scoped subtasks IN PARALLEL as worker agents, each optionally on its own model/provider, and get all results back. Use to fan a goal across workers — research three things at once, or run one task on local ollama and a harder one on gpt-4o simultaneously.

tools.name: council
tools.description: Convene a bounded role council (CEO/CTO/COO/CFO + a Reflection role) on one question. Each role deliberates from its lens in a single pass, then the Reflection role synthesizes them into ONE consolidated recommendation. The roster is fixed and capped — no recursion. Use for a multi-perspective decision (ship/no-ship, build-vs-buy, strategy calls).

question.description: The decision/question the council deliberates on

tools.name: generate_agent
tools.description: Generate a new agent definition (identifier + when-to-use + system prompt) from a plain-English description, tailored using repository context, and write it to an agent file under the Vanta home. Use this to create a reusable specialist agent the orchestrator can later delegate to.

description.description: Plain-English description of the agent to create (its purpose and behavior)

repo_context.description: Optional repository/stack context to tailor the agent (e.g. languages, conventions)

tools.name: compose_workflow
tools.description: Compose, diff, and run declarative agent workflow graphs. Supports agent, approval, and interview nodes plus next, branch, loop, and parallel transitions. Also accepts the legacy typed step sequence.

mode.description: Default run. Use diff with previous_spec.

previous_spec.description: Previous graph spec for stable diff output.

tools.name: graph_query
tools.description: Query the knowledge graph for entities and their relationships. Returns matching entities with their direct connections (worked-on, decided, depends-on, related-to, etc.).

tools.name: transcribe
tools.description: Transcribe an audio file to text (speech-to-text via whisper). Args: path, model (default base).

path.description: Path to an audio file (mp3/wav/m4a/…)

tools.name: twitter_read
tools.description: Search X/Twitter or list your bookmarks — authenticated GraphQL, no external CLI, keyless cookie auth. action:search {query, max?, latest?} finds tweets; action:bookmarks {max?} lists your saved tweets. Needs an x.com cookie (cookie_import channel "twitter") + current query ids (reach heal twitter). Source-cited.

latest.description: search: newest first instead of top

tools.name: youtube_read
tools.description: Extract info and/or subtitles from a YouTube video via yt-dlp. mode=info returns title/description/metadata; mode=subtitles returns the caption text; mode=both (default) returns everything available. Zero-config if yt-dlp is installed.

mode.description: What to extract — both is default

tools.name: github_read
tools.description: Read GitHub repos, issues, PRs, and READMEs via gh CLI. Zero-config for public repos. Run gh auth login to unlock private repos, fork, issue, PR creation.

action.description: repo=view details, issues=list open issues, prs=list open PRs, readme=read README, search=search GitHub

repo.description: owner/repo or full GitHub URL (not needed for search)

tools.name: podcast_read
tools.description: Transcribe a podcast episode or audio file via Groq Whisper (whisper-large-v3). Pass a direct audio URL (.mp3/.m4a etc.). Requires GROQ_API_KEY (free at console.groq.com). Audio must be ≤24 MB.

tools.name: v2ex_read
tools.description: Read V2EX public community data with no auth. Actions: hot, latest, node {node}, topic {topicId}, replies {topicId}, member {username}.

node.description: V2EX node name for action=node, e.g. python

tools.name: bilibili_read
tools.description: Search Bilibili videos and read video detail through bili-cli when installed, with Bilibili's public search API as a search-only fallback. Subtitles use OpenCLI when configured. Actions: search {query, limit?}, video {url|bvid}, subtitles {url|bvid}.

tools.name: xueqiu_read
tools.description: Read Xueqiu stock quotes, stock search, hot posts, and hot-stock ranking using a stored logged-in xueqiu cookie. Actions: quote {symbol}, search {query, limit?}, hot_posts {limit?}, hot_stocks {limit?, type?}.

symbol.description: quote symbol, e.g. SH600519, SZ000858, AAPL, 00700

query.description: stock code or name for action=search

tools.name: xiaohongshu_read
tools.description: Read Xiaohongshu through a configured logged-in OpenCLI backend. Actions: search {query}, note {url|noteId}, comments {url|noteId}, feed {}.

tools.name: run_code
tools.description: Run a code snippet (python, node, or rust) in an isolated temp dir with a 30s timeout. Returns combined stdout/stderr. Requires approval.

language.description: The language to run the snippet in

tools.name: run_maximizer
tools.description: Maximizer mode: higher-autonomy execution under a HARD budget. Delegates each task in `tasks` to a worker (kernel-gated), follows through across all of them, records a visible activity trail, and STOPS the moment cumulative spend reaches `budgetUsd`. Use it to get more verified output per supervisor — it is bounded autonomy, not a blank check.

tasks.description: ordered tasks to delegate and follow through

budgetUsd.description: hard USD spend cap for the whole run; execution stops when reached

tools.name: lsp_diagnostics
tools.description: Report TypeScript diagnostics (errors and warnings) for a .ts/.tsx file inside the project scope.

path.description: Path to a .ts/.tsx file relative to the project root

tools.name: lsp_definition
tools.description: Find the definition site(s) of the symbol at a position in a .ts/.tsx file inside the project scope.

path.description: Path to a .ts/.tsx file relative to the project root

character.description: Zero-based character offset of the symbol

tools.name: lsp_references
tools.description: Find every reference to the symbol at a position in a .ts/.tsx file inside the project scope.

path.description: Path to a .ts/.tsx file relative to the project root

character.description: Zero-based character offset of the symbol

tools.name: lsp_symbols
tools.description: List the document symbols (declarations) of a .ts/.tsx file inside the project scope.

path.description: Path to a .ts/.tsx file relative to the project root

tools.name: lsp_hover
tools.description: Show the type/signature (quick-info) for the symbol at a position in a .ts/.tsx file inside the project scope.

path.description: Path to a .ts/.tsx file relative to the project root

character.description: Zero-based character offset of the symbol

tools.name: list_mcp_resources
tools.description: List all resources exposed by mounted MCP servers. Returns resource URIs and descriptions. Resources are file-like content provided by MCP servers (e.g., API docs, code files, logs).

tools.name: read_mcp_resource
tools.description: Read the content of a resource from a mounted MCP server. Requires the full resource URI (available via list_mcp_resources).

tools.name: git_status
tools.description: Show working-tree status (porcelain) with branch info.

tools.name: git_diff
tools.description: Show unstaged changes, optionally limited to one path.

path.description: Optional path to diff

tools.name: git_commit
tools.description: Stage all changes and commit with a message. Requires approval.

tools.name: git_push
tools.description: Push commits to a remote. Requires approval.

tools.name: git_branch
tools.description: Create a branch by name, or list branches when no name given. Requires approval.

tools.name: git_checkout
tools.description: Check out a branch, tag, or commit ref. Requires approval.

ref.description: Branch, tag, or commit to check out

tools.name: delegate
tools.description: Delegate a scoped subtask to a worker agent — optionally on a DIFFERENT model/provider. The worker runs its own loop with the same tools (minus delegate) and returns its result. Use `provider`/`model` to route a subtask to the best backend (e.g. provider:'ollama' for a free local model, provider:'openai' model:'gpt-4o' for a hard reasoning step). Call it multiple times to fan a goal out across several workers/models.

goal.description: The worker's scoped goal — the outcome to achieve

instruction.description: Concrete instructions for the worker to follow

max_iterations.description: Optional cap on the worker's loop iterations (1-50)

provider.description: Optional backend for the worker: openai | ollama | anthropic | gemini | openrouter | codex | claude-code. Defaults to the parent's.

model.description: Optional model id for the worker (e.g. gpt-4o, qwen2.5:14b, gemini-2.5-flash).

isolation.description: Set to 'worktree' to run the agent in a fresh git worktree on a new branch so parallel agents don't conflict.

background.description: Run the worker in the BACKGROUND: the call returns immediately and the worker's result re-enters as a new turn when the session is idle. Use for long subtasks you don't need to block on.

tools.name: call_agent
tools.description: Call ANOTHER AI coding-agent CLI non-interactively (agent-to-agent: headless, no terminal) and return its result. Auto-detects whatever is installed (claude, codex, gemini, cursor-agent, opencode out of the box; ANY other CLI/harness declared in ~/.vanta/agents.json). Call with no agent (or agent='list') to list. Pass coding:true to delegate BUILDING — the agent runs build-ready (auto-accepts file edits) and actually writes/changes code, then returns what it did; use coding:true whenever the user wants the other agent to build/implement/fix/create code (without it, the agent can only answer, not edit files). Otherwise pass {agent, prompt, model?}. The called agent runs in its own harness. Use to delegate a build, get a second model's take, or cross-check.

agent.description: Which agent CLI to call (e.g. claude, codex, gemini). Omit or 'list' to list detected agents.

prompt.description: The prompt/task to send to the agent

model.description: Optional model override passed through to that agent's CLI

coding.description: Delegate BUILDING: the agent auto-accepts file edits so it can write/change code headless. Default false (answer-only).

autonomous.description: FULL autonomy, OS-contained: runs the agent with --dangerously-skip-permissions inside a Docker container scoped to exactly this project (rw) + its auth (ro), network on only for the model API. The container is the boundary — it provably cannot touch any other host path. For hands-free builds you want boxed. claude only; needs Docker — run `vanta agent-image build` once to set up the container image (override with VANTA_AGENT_DOCKER_IMAGE).

tools.name: agent_session
tools.description: Open a PERSISTENT interactive session over another agent CLI (claude/codex/gemini/cursor-agent/opencode) and drive it turn-by-turn — unlike call_agent (one-shot, headless), this keeps its conversation context AND opens a VISIBLE terminal window the user can watch the agent work in. Use this (not call_agent) when the user says open/start/watch a session or wants to see it. Pass coding:true to launch it BUILD-READY (auto-accepts file edits so it can actually write/change code hands-free) — use that when the user wants the agent to build/implement/fix code, not just chat. Actions: open {agent, coding?} → id (pops a window; pass show:false for headless); send {id, text} (returns the agent's reply); read {id} (re-read the pane); close {id}; list. Backed by a tmux session.

id.description: For send/read/close: the session id from open

text.description: For send: the prompt to send to the agent

show.description: For open: open a visible terminal window to watch (default true; false = headless)

coding.description: For open: launch build-ready (auto-accepts file edits so the agent can write/change code hands-free). Default false.

tools.name: build_with_agent
tools.description: Delegate a BUILD to another coding agent and CLOSE THE LOOP: it builds (coding mode, streams progress), then Vanta VERIFIES (the expectFiles exist + an optional verifyCmd exits 0), and re-delegates a targeted fix if verification fails — up to maxIters. Use this (over a bare call_agent) when the user wants something built and actually working. Pass {agent, task, expectFiles?, verifyCmd?, maxIters?}.

agent.description: Which agent CLI builds it (e.g. claude)

expectFiles.description: Files that must exist after a successful build (relative to cwd)

verifyCmd.description: Optional shell command that must exit 0 to count as verified (e.g. 'npm test', 'node check.js')

tools.name: gmail_search
tools.description: Search the user's Gmail with a Gmail query string. Returns matching message ids with sender, subject, and snippet.

tools.name: gmail_read
tools.description: Read a single Gmail message by id. Returns its headers and plain-text body.

tools.name: gmail_draft
tools.description: Create a Gmail draft (does not send). Requires human approval.

tools.name: gmail_send
tools.description: Send an email from the user's account. Irreversible. Requires human approval.

tools.name: calendar_read
tools.description: List upcoming events from the user's primary Google calendar, ordered by start time.

tools.name: calendar_create
tools.description: Create an event on the user's primary Google calendar. Always requires approval.

tools.name: calendar_update
tools.description: Update fields of an existing event on the primary Google calendar. Always requires approval.

tools.name: drive_read
tools.description: Read a Google Drive file's text content by file id. Falls back to plain-text export for Google-native docs.

tools.name: drive_create
tools.description: Create a new file in Google Drive with the given name and text content. Always requires approval.

tools.name: drive_update
tools.description: Replace the content of an existing Google Drive file by id. Always requires approval.

tools.name: google_auth
tools.description: Authorize Vanta with Google. Two steps: 1) Call with action='start' — returns the consent URL; show it to the user. 2) Call with action='complete' — waits (up to 5 min) for the user to approve in their browser, then saves the tokens. Use when the user says 'auth google' or 'vanta auth google'. Do NOT shell out to ./run.sh auth google.

action.description: 'start' returns the consent URL. 'complete' polls for the callback and saves tokens.

tools.name: marketing_read
tools.description: Read marketing/analytics records from Amplitude events or Customer.io campaigns. Uses env credentials for live reads or a fixture path for review/test runs.

fixture.description: Optional local JSON fixture path instead of a live API read.

tools.name: render_canvas
tools.description: Render a bounded interactive chart, table, or board in the Vanta Desktop Canvas. Replaces the current canvas artifact.

title.description: Visible artifact title, at most 120 characters.

subtitle.description: Optional visible context, at most 240 characters.

items.description: Object keyed by column key; values must be string, number, boolean, or null.

tools.name: media_studio
tools.description: Preview or approval-gated render a scoped local MP4 from bounded color/image scenes. FFmpeg/ffprobe verify duration, dimensions, streams, bytes, and a nonblank frame; receipts retain sources, provider, cost, and checks.

brief.description: Media brief: title, relative .mp4 output, dimensions/fps, and 1-24 color or project-image scenes.

tools.name: payment_transaction
tools.description: Preview or execute a strict test-gated payment contract. Exact totals, caps, expiry, replay protection, fresh operator approval, provider approval, redacted receipts, and HTTP 402 validation are mandatory. Never accepts card data, API keys, or plaintext credentials.

contract.description: Strict version-1 payment contract. Use minor units and provider_cli credential storage only.

tools.name: shopify_operations
tools.description: Read bounded products/orders/inventory or preview and fresh-approval-gate typed product/inventory mutations. Store tokens resolve from scoped vault aliases and never enter arguments or receipts.

tools.name: telephony_workflow
tools.description: Search test numbers or preview/execute consented Twilio SMS, bounded calls, and number provisioning. Requires explicit purpose, consent, time window, recording/retention choice, idempotency, fresh approval, and lifecycle receipts.

tools.name: spreadsheet_workbook
tools.description: Inspect, preview, or approval-gated apply cell, formula, and sheet changes to a scoped local .xlsx workbook. Apply reopens the result and writes a SHA-256 receipt.

items.description: Change: set/formula requires sheet+cell; add_sheet/delete_sheet requires sheet.

tools.name: bg_list
tools.description: List background shell tasks spawned this session. Optionally filter by status (all|running|done|failed).

tools.name: bg_status
tools.description: Check the status and optionally tail the output log of a background task.

id.description: Task id from shell_cmd background run

log.description: Include the last 4000 chars of output (default false)

tools.name: ref_ingest
tools.description: Ingest a reference (URL / file / repo / image / transcript) into durable project context. Stored under ~/.vanta/refs/ and recallable across sessions without re-pasting. Pass an excerpt to skip fetching; or let the tool read the source.

source.description: URL, file path, or repo path to ingest

tools.name: ref_search
tools.description: Search ingested references by keyword. Returns matching refs with their excerpts.

tools.name: ref_list
tools.description: List all ingested references, most recent first.

tools.name: protect
tools.description: Scan text for threats: scams, credential exposure, destructive commands, social engineering, agent-overreach instructions, and contract traps. Use on suspicious messages, contract clauses, or any input that might be risky.

tools.name: retrieve_original
tools.description: Expand a compressed tool output back to its full original. Pass the original_id shown in a [vanta compressed …] footer to read the complete content.

original_id.description: The original_id from a [vanta compressed …] footer.

tools.name: loop
tools.description: Create and manage first-class loops: durable, goal-driven iteration cycles that run stages (discover/plan/execute/evaluate/improve) on a trigger (heartbeat/cron/manual). Use add to register, list/show to inspect, pause/resume/kill to control status, run to fire one iteration as a background process (non-blocking), and escalations to read open blockers. Escalations are surfaced here but only a human can clear them via `vanta loop clear` — the agent must not resolve its own blockers.

goal.description: add: natural-language goal the loop pursues.

trigger.description: add: trigger spec — manual | heartbeat | heartbeat:<N> | cron:"<expr>".

purge.description: kill: if true, delete files instead of marking killed.

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Send a message to a named agent registered on the A2A bus. Returns the agent's reply, or a delivery note when the agent returns no reply.

to.description: The agent id to send to.

tools.name: send_chat
tools.description: Proactively send a message to a configured chat platform (e.g. telegram) — works WITHOUT the gateway running. Resolves the platform's adapter, connects, sends one message, and disconnects. Use to push an update to a chat from a cron/loop wake. Outbound — approval-gated. Implemented platforms: telegram, mattermost, irc, ntfy, imessage, signal, whatsapp, slack, discord, matrix, line, teams, twitch, sms, zalo, feishu, qq, wechat, webchat, nostr, googlechat, email.

chatId.description: Platform-specific conversation id to send to

tools.name: playbook
tools.description: Cross-session experiential playbook. record: capture a reusable strategy after completing a task. recall: surface matching strategies from prior sessions before tackling a task. list: browse recent plays.

tools.name: enter_worktree
tools.description: Create an isolated git worktree (its own branch + directory) for parallel work without touching the main checkout. Returns the worktree path and branch; clean it up afterwards with exit_worktree.

tools.name: exit_worktree
tools.description: Remove a git worktree created by enter_worktree. Auto-cleans (drops the worktree directory and its branch) only when it has NO uncommitted changes. If it is dirty, refuses and surfaces the changes — pass force:true to discard them and remove anyway.

path.description: Worktree directory path (from enter_worktree)

force.description: Discard uncommitted changes and remove anyway (default false)

tools.name: list_peers
tools.description: List other Vanta sessions running on this machine (peer agents discovered over Unix domain sockets). Returns each live peer's id, title, and pid. Use peer_send with a peer's id to collaborate across sessions.

tools.name: peer_send
tools.description: Send a message to another Vanta session over a Unix domain socket. Pass the target peer's id (from list_peers) and the text; it is appended to that peer's inbox. Returns delivered or failed.

to.description: The peer agent id to send to (from list_peers).

tools.name: open_deep_link
tools.description: Parse and resolve a vanta:// deep link into a safe launch descriptor for a pre-filled Vanta session. Accepts vanta://run?prompt=...&cwd=...&repo=... — URL-decodes the params, rejects control characters and non-path cwd/repo, and returns the resolved argv (never a shell string). On macOS it may also open a terminal for a fully-validated link; the descriptor is the deliverable.

url.description: The vanta:// deep link, e.g. vanta://run?prompt=fix%20auth&cwd=/repo.

tools.name: mount_mcp
tools.description: Spawn an MCP server process and mount its tools into the active registry. Use to hook in an existing MCP server or one you just scaffolded. Returns the list of tool names registered.

name.description: Unique name for this server (used as tool name prefix mcp_<name>_<tool>)

command.description: Command to spawn the server (e.g. npx, node)

args.description: Arguments to pass to the command

env.description: Optional env vars for the server process

tools.name: mcp_auth
tools.description: Authorize an MCP server that requires OAuth. Call with the server name to get an authorization URL — give it to the user to open and approve. After they authorize, call mcp_auth again for the same server to reconnect it and make its tools available.

server.description: Name of the MCP server to authorize (as configured).

tools.name: tool_search
tools.description: Search for tools by name or description keyword. Returns matching tool names + full schemas. Use before calling an unfamiliar tool to verify its parameter shape and make the result callable on the next turn.

query.description: Search query (tool name substring or keyword)

maxResults.description: Max number of results to return (default 5, max 20)

tools.name: run_pipeline
tools.description: Run a linear, deterministic chain of tool calls in ONE turn. Each step calls a tool; bind a step's output with `assignTo` and reference it in a later step's `args` via `$name` or `{{name}}`. Only the FINAL step's result returns to you — intermediate outputs stay in bindings, costing ~zero context. Every step is kernel-gated like a direct call. Use for fetch→transform→write chains where you don't need to read each intermediate. Example: steps=[{tool:"read_file",args:{path:"a.json"},assignTo:"raw"},{tool:"run_code",args:{lang:"python",code:"...$raw..."},assignTo:"clean"},{tool:"write_file",args:{path:"b.json",content:"{{clean}}"}}].

steps.description: ordered tool calls; each {tool, args, assignTo?}

args.description: tool args; $name / {{name}} reference a prior assignTo

assignTo.description: bind this output to a name (kept out of context)

commands.name: help
commands.desc: show this command list

commands.name: home
commands.desc: operator home — workflows, channels, skills, tasks, memory, watchers, setup

commands.name: blueprint
commands.desc: preview a form-driven schedule or webhook automation

commands.name: what-can-i-do
commands.desc: show concrete runnable workflows for this Vanta install

commands.name: diagnose-crash
commands.desc: diagnose a pasted macOS/iOS/build crash log with cited evidence

commands.name: spec-to-app
commands.desc: build a verified React/Tailwind preview from a product spec

commands.name: autonomy
commands.desc: show the acts-alone / queues / wakes-me contract

commands.name: clear
commands.desc: start a fresh conversation (keeps the session log)

commands.name: reset
commands.desc: start a fresh conversation (alias of /clear)

commands.name: history
commands.desc: show this conversation's transcript

commands.name: export
commands.desc: export this conversation — format, tool/thinking toggles, file or clipboard

commands.name: init
commands.desc: generate .claude/CLAUDE.md project context for future sessions

commands.name: init-verifiers
commands.desc: detect the project's build/test/lint/typecheck gates → verifier skills it can run to check its work

commands.name: stop
commands.desc: graceful soft-stop — finish the current tool call, then end the turn with a summary

commands.name: bg
commands.desc: detach/check the active response in the background (TUI)

commands.name: retry
commands.desc: re-run your last message

commands.name: undo
commands.desc: drop the last turn from the conversation

commands.name: rewind
commands.desc: list or restore recent per-edit file checkpoints

commands.name: checkpoint
commands.desc: snapshot the full conversation state under a name

commands.name: restore
commands.desc: restore a checkpoint in place, or branch it into a new session

commands.name: hooks
commands.desc: list, add, or remove shell hooks in .vanta/hooks.json

commands.name: model
commands.desc: change provider & model — interactive picker

commands.name: effort
commands.desc: set model effort for this session

commands.name: env
commands.desc: session-scoped env vars injected into shell_cmd/run_code child processes

commands.name: cd
commands.desc: change the session working directory for shell_cmd (no arg prints it)

commands.name: setup
commands.desc: configure the model backend — opens the provider/model picker

commands.name: tools
commands.desc: list available tools

commands.name: skills
commands.desc: list skills, audit injection flags, or review staged agent skill changes

commands.name: skillify
commands.desc: distill this session into a draft SKILL.md (write_skill saves it — not auto-written)

commands.name: learn
commands.desc: build a skill from a doc/URL — distills, gates, and saves an editable SKILL.md

commands.name: status
commands.desc: kernel, provider, keys, store health

commands.name: health
commands.desc: capability health — gmail/search/vision/browser/mcp + the exact fix for each gap

commands.name: world
commands.desc: view Vanta's world model — entities + relationships across your systems

commands.name: money
commands.desc: Money OS ledger — offers, prospect pipeline, revenue total

commands.name: radar
commands.desc: Opportunity radar — scored opportunities ranked by pain + buyer signal

commands.name: team
commands.desc: background worker roster — named agents, roles, status, blockers

commands.name: teams
commands.desc: interactive team roster — create workers, manage status, inspect assigned tasks

commands.name: peers
commands.desc: live Vanta peer sessions on this machine (UDS) — id, title, pid for cross-session collab

commands.name: lifesearch
commands.desc: search across Vanta's local stores (world/money/radar/team/errors) — source-cited

commands.name: compartments
commands.desc: Vanta's self-repair body map — compartments + max autonomy per part

commands.name: skeptic
commands.desc: adversarially verify a claim — refute by default, demand evidence

commands.name: security-review
commands.desc: security audit of the current branch's diff vs base (injection/secret/authz/traversal/exec/SSRF)

commands.name: locks
commands.desc: regression locks — verified behaviors + passing/regressed status (verification organ)

commands.name: nd
commands.desc: executive-function support gates — view/toggle/tune the ND gate set

commands.name: reach
commands.desc: internet-reach doctor — each channel's active backend + status + the exact fix per gap

commands.name: cookie
commands.desc: show login-walled reach channels with a stored cookie + the export guide

commands.name: goals
commands.desc: active goals from the kernel

commands.name: goal
commands.desc: set / resume / drop a standing goal (a carried goal starts paused)

commands.name: plan
commands.desc: show the agent's current task plan (todo list)

commands.name: memory
commands.desc: browse memory files, or save a fact to Vanta's brain

commands.name: learnings
commands.desc: per-project learnings index — relevant insights, stale/conflicting flagged

commands.name: compress
commands.desc: compact the conversation context now

commands.name: compact
commands.desc: compact context now (alias of /compress; optional steer)

commands.name: sessions
commands.desc: list saved sessions

commands.name: searchall
commands.desc: search across all saved sessions (TUI)

commands.name: resume
commands.desc: load a past session into this conversation

commands.name: title
commands.desc: name the current session

commands.name: fork
commands.desc: branch the current conversation into a new session

commands.name: cron
commands.desc: list scheduled tasks

commands.name: loop
commands.desc: schedule a recurring task from a natural-language interval (e.g. every 2 hours <task>, daily, every monday)

commands.name: activity
commands.desc: who/what/why timeline over the event log — /activity [--who tool] [--kind gate|tool|note] [--risk ask|blocked] [--since 2h] [--limit N] [text]

commands.name: proactive
commands.desc: proactive-autonomy mode (KAIROS) — whether idle ticking is enabled, the throttle, and would-it-tick-now (read-only)

commands.name: image
commands.desc: attach an image for your next message

commands.name: paste
commands.desc: attach an image from the clipboard (macOS)

commands.name: attachments
commands.desc: show or clear pending image attachments

commands.name: branch
commands.desc: create or switch git branch (kernel-gated)

commands.name: context
commands.desc: visual context-budget breakdown

commands.name: files
commands.desc: list files in the current conversation context

commands.name: composer
commands.desc: input box position: float (default) or bottom-pinned

commands.name: vim
commands.desc: toggle vi-mode in the composer (normal/insert: hjkl, w/b, dd, yy, p, i/a/o)

commands.name: terminal-setup
commands.desc: print the steps to bind Shift+Enter → newline for your detected terminal (iTerm2/Apple Terminal/VS Code/WezTerm)

commands.name: cockpit
commands.desc: open mission-control — kernel verdict ladder, goals, loops (TUI)

commands.name: loops
commands.desc: live loops + escalations dashboard (TUI)

commands.name: changes
commands.desc: review changed files — per-file keep/undo (TUI)

commands.name: agents
commands.desc: custom agent editor — model, tools, color, markdown file (TUI)

commands.name: mcp
commands.desc: MCP panel — servers + connection status, per-server tools, tool detail, reconnect (TUI)

commands.name: sandbox
commands.desc: sandbox settings — config, dependencies, doctor, per-tool overrides (TUI)

commands.name: permissions
commands.desc: tighten-only tool permission rules (~/.vanta/permissions.tsv)

commands.name: less-permission-prompts
commands.desc: scan the session for repeatedly-approved read-only tools and propose allow rules to cut future prompts (propose-only)

commands.name: reload-plugins
commands.desc: re-scan enabled plugins and load any added this session — reports newly available vs already loaded

commands.name: plugin-panels
commands.desc: open data-only panels contributed by isolated plugin workers (TUI)

commands.name: reload-skills
commands.desc: re-scan skill directories and pick up any added this session — reports added vs already-indexed vs removed

commands.name: preferences
commands.desc: inspect and correct Vanta's operator beliefs

commands.name: usage
commands.desc: token usage + context fill for this session

commands.name: stats
commands.desc: aggregate usage — sessions, turns, tool calls, tokens/cost (TUI)

commands.name: copy
commands.desc: copy the last response to the clipboard

commands.name: screenshot
commands.desc: copy the current Vanta transcript to clipboard as a PNG image

commands.name: update
commands.desc: git pull the latest Vanta (then ./install.sh to rebuild)

commands.name: moim
commands.desc: pin a top-of-mind note injected into every prompt until cleared

commands.name: record
commands.desc: record terminal output to an asciicast v2 .cast file under ~/.vanta/recordings

commands.name: output-style
commands.desc: control response verbosity

commands.name: ultrathink
commands.desc: deep-reasoning mode — plan, weigh tradeoffs + edge cases, then act

commands.name: ultracode
commands.desc: multi-agent coding push — decompose, parallel subagents, verify, synthesize

commands.name: deep-research
commands.desc: fan-out research — multi-source search, skeptic-verify, cited synthesis

commands.name: next
commands.desc: surface one concrete next micro-step from your active goals

commands.name: suggest
commands.desc: recap + ranked next-step (top 3); '/suggest all' for the full backlog

commands.name: time
commands.desc: session elapsed + time since last action (ND-TIME-RANGES)

commands.name: now
commands.desc: execute items in the Now column — agent picks up whatever you put there

commands.name: planmode
commands.desc: enforced plan mode — write tools blocked until /planmode approve

commands.name: planv2
commands.desc: plan mode v2 — fan a task out across N concurrent plan-execution agents (VANTA_PLAN_V2_AGENT_COUNT, 1-10)

commands.name: tickets
commands.desc: issue board — first-class tickets grouped by status, with goal/parent links + inbox state

commands.name: outreach
commands.desc: authorized brand workspace — pending drafts + the proof ledger (draft-only, approval-gated)

commands.name: boundary
commands.desc: mark a task boundary — archive the current task state and begin fresh

commands.name: where
commands.desc: show last stated intent and recent tool call breadcrumb

commands.name: explain
commands.desc: capability-preservation surface — what changed + why, with a comprehension probe on risky/large changes

commands.name: recover
commands.desc: classify trouble — targeted bug, polluted context, or wrong assumption

commands.name: wm
commands.desc: view or add to session working memory (cleared each session)

commands.name: restart
commands.desc: reload Vanta in place with fresh code (needs ./run.sh)

commands.name: bug
commands.desc: record a structured bug with session + git context

commands.name: feedback
commands.desc: draft a redacted GitHub issue from feedback/feature requests; `send` files it

commands.name: learning
commands.desc: self-learning loop status — skills minted/refined/adopted + adoption rate

commands.name: handoff
commands.desc: copy-paste handoff packet (goals, git, files, next step)

commands.name: open
commands.desc: open a file:line in your editor ($VANTA_EDITOR/code)

commands.name: edit
commands.desc: edit the last AI response in your editor ($VANTA_EDITOR/code)

commands.name: tasks
commands.desc: operator task stack — /tasks next for the best move

commands.name: bgtasks
commands.desc: background shell tasks — list status, stop one by id

commands.name: wftasks
commands.desc: workflow run task list — compose_workflow runs + their running/done/failed status

commands.name: workflow-select
commands.desc: choose, skip, reorder, and run steps from .vanta/workflow-draft.json

commands.name: btw
commands.desc: ask a quick side question — not added to conversation history

commands.name: describe
commands.desc: generate a short LLM description of a file or directory

commands.name: diff
commands.desc: show uncommitted changes (working tree + staged)

commands.name: search
commands.desc: full-text search this session's transcript — ranked matches + highlighted snippets

commands.name: repro
commands.desc: save a diagnostic repro bundle to .vanta/repro-*.md

commands.name: summary
commands.desc: summarize this session (what was done, what remains)

commands.name: dashboard
commands.desc: live operator state — tasks, goals, repo, model

commands.name: brief
commands.desc: JARVIS-style today brief — tasks, goals, calendar, episodic

commands.name: review
commands.desc: review changed code for bugs and cleanups (low|medium|high)

commands.name: simplify
commands.desc: reuse/simplify/efficiency/altitude pass on changed code

commands.name: auto
commands.desc: auto-minimalism mode — do the least that works (stdlib>deps, deletion>addition); 'review' audits a diff for deletable code

commands.name: verify
commands.desc: run the app and confirm a change actually works

commands.name: run
commands.desc: launch and drive this project's app

commands.name: add-dir
commands.desc: add a directory to this session's readable/writable scope

commands.name: routes
commands.desc: show provider routing config (main + named VANTA_ROUTE_* overrides)

commands.name: rename
commands.desc: rename the current session

commands.name: tui
commands.desc: TUI renderer info; /tui fullscreen confirms alt-screen mode

commands.name: focus
commands.desc: toggle focus view — hide tool events, show only user + final responses

commands.name: config
commands.desc: interactive settings — view + change config (TUI)

commands.name: settings
commands.desc: show/edit persisted settings

commands.name: models
commands.desc: list available models for the active provider

commands.name: lint
commands.desc: run the code-size gate on changed files

commands.name: roadmap
commands.desc: open the drag-and-drop roadmap board

commands.name: audit
commands.desc: run npm audit + dependency checks

commands.name: today
commands.desc: today brief — tasks, goals, calendar, episodic

commands.name: import
commands.desc: import config/skills from a backup archive

commands.name: exit
commands.desc: leave the session
